item.title: Tulfo confirms junking of quo warranto case against him before SET
item.description: Senator Erwin Tulfo on Wednesday thanked the Senate Electoral Tribunal (SET) for junking the quo warranto petition filed against him, a petition that challenged his right to hold public office because of alleged ineligibility arising from his citizenship.

item.title: DSWD, LTFRB iron out list of cash aid beneficiaries as more drivers complain of being left out
item.description: Jeepney driver Zaldy Manalo lined up early Wednesday at the Quezon Memorial Circle for the cash aid payout from the Department of Social Welfare and Development and the Land Franchising and Regulatory Board, only to find out that he was not included in the master list of beneficiaries.

item.title: 5 suspects arrested for carnapping, illegal firearms and explosives in Cavite
item.description: Five individuals were arrested in a hot pursuit operation after allegedly being involved in carnapping and illegal possession of firearms, explosives, and ammunition in Tanza early Wednesday morning.

item.title: Various groups to join 2-day nationwide transport strike over fuel price surge
item.description: A coalition of transport groups, urban poor organizations, and progressive groups will join a two-day nationwide transport strike starting Thursday to protest rising fuel prices and what they describe as the government’s inadequate response to the crisis.

item.title: Gatchalian calls for unified national contingency plan to address oil crisis
item.description: Senate Committee on Finance Chairperson Sherwin Gatchalian called for a unified, coordinated national contingency plan to address the oil crisis brought about by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
